article_meta.title: Elections Unsealed Court Docs Show The FBI Probing Possible Election Crimes In Fulton County By: M.D

reasoning: The model does a good job identifying genuine bias patterns in the author's own prose. All flagged items are from the author's connecting prose, not from quoted sources. The bias type labels are appropriate. There are a few additional instances that could have been flagged (e.g., 'smearing witnesses who raise legitimate concerns,' 'election deniers' framing by the author, 'Fulton County apologists,' 'Democrat-controlled county,' 'Zuckbucks campaign,' 'If the Democrat-controlled county...has done nothing wrong, it certainly has nothing to hide'), but the model correctly identified the most prominent instances. The model showed good restraint in not flagging quoted text.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All six flagged items are clearly from the author's own prose. No quoted text or attributed source language was flagged. Perfect attribution rule compliance.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All flagged items are defensible instances of author-level bias. 'Propaganda press,' 'Trump-hating publication,' 'curious discrepancies,' 'curiously left out,' and the treatment of David Becker are all genuine bias patterns. The Slant item about The Post's omissions is slightly more debatable but still defensible.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Several significant author-level bias instances were missed: 'smearing witnesses who raise legitimate concerns' (loaded framing), 'Fulton County apologists' (pejorative label), 'Democrat-controlled county' (loaded framing), 'Zuckbucks campaign that opened the door to Democrat operatives and leftist nonprofits infiltrating local election administration' (loaded language), 'If the Democrat-controlled county...has done nothing wrong, it certainly has nothing to hide' (rhetorical bias). These are notable misses but the model caught the most prominent examples.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are well-applied. 'Word Choice' is correct for 'propaganda press' and 'Trump-hating publication.' 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' fits 'curious discrepancies.' 'Slant' is reasonable for the structural framing issues. One might argue some items could also be labeled as Spin or Mudslinging, but the chosen labels are defensible.
